---
title: "Summaries"
author: "Robert Esposito"
format: pdf
---

# @sole1991differentiating

Spanish vowel nasalization varied with F1 (height) and possibly F2 (backness); in American English, coarticulatory nasalization varies inversely with speech rate. This indicates that Spanish vowel nasalization is phonetic, whereas AE vowel nasalization is phonemic. They used nasometer. Modeled Spanish & AE vowel nasalzation:

American English:

```
a = d
```

Multiplicative effect (multiplied by a factor of 1).

Spanish:

```
k(v):
a=k(v)
b=d-k(v)
```

Where k(v) is a constant value (incompressible beyond 40 ms) which depends on height of vowel.

b = oral portion of the vowel

d = total duration of the vowel sequence

# 

# References

::: {#refs}
:::
